Title: The family of terpene synthases in plants: a mid-size family of genes for specialized metabolism that is highly diversified throughout the kingdom

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase (TPS-c clade); bifunctional class II/class I diterpene synthase with DXDD and DDXXD motifs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts geranylgeranyl diphosphate (GGPP) to copalyl diphosphate and then to ent-kaurene; also forms 16-hydroxy-ent-kaurene in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: whole plant; 16-hydroxy-ent-kaurene accumulates in Physcomitrella patens tissues and is also released as a volatile
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: developmental regulation in land plants; possible ecological signaling/interaction role for 16-hydroxy-ent-kaurene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-kaurene and 16-hydroxy-ent-kaurene, supporting endogenous diterpene metabolism linked to development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene functional characterization; in vitro enzyme assay; metabolite analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCPS/KS is the single functional TPS gene identified in Physcomitrella patens. 2. It is bifunctional and catalyzes formation of ent-kaurene and 16-hydroxy-ent-kaurene from GGPP. 3. ent-Kaurene-derived metabolites act in endogenous developmental regulation, indicating an ancestral diterpene metabolic role.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an evolutionary model for engineering bifunctional diterpene synthases and gibberellin-precursor pathways; direct crop breeding value not discussed
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: comparative genomics / phylogenetic analysis; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper; evidence summarized from Hayashi et al. 2006, 2010 and related studies. The gene is treated as a core summarized pathway gene because it is a major exemplar in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; class II diterpene synthase with DXDD motif; TPS-c clade
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to copalyl diphosphate (CPP) through protonation-initiated cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: copalyl diphosphate (CPP; including ent-CPP or syn-CPP depending on isoform)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control/committed step for diterpene branch entry, as summarized
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: growth regulation for gibberellins; defense for specialized diterpenoids in some species
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables conversion of GGPP into CPP, thereby supporting gibberellin and specialized diterpene formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization in multiple species; enzyme assays; phylogenetic comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPS represents the class II diterpene synthase branch of the TPS family. 2. It converts GGPP to CPP, supplying precursors for gibberellins and specialized diterpenoids. 3. Plant CPS genes are evolutionarily derived from ancestral bifunctional CPS/KS genes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter gibberellin levels or defensive diterpene production depending on isoform/species
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: comparative genomics / phylogenetic analysis; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may alter growth through gibberellin metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Generic summarized core pathway gene class included because the review centers on TPS family functions.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; class I diterpene synthase with DDXXD motif; TPS-e clade
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-kaurene, the direct precursor for all plant gibberellins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization in multiple species; enzyme assays; phylogenetic comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KS is the class I diterpene synthase in primary gibberellin metabolism. 2. It converts CPP to ent-kaurene in angiosperms and gymnosperms. 3. KS genes likely arose from ancestral bifunctional CPS/KS genes through duplication and subfunctionalization.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to modulate gibberellin precursor flux, but growth tradeoffs are expected
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: comparative genomics / phylogenetic analysis; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may impact plant growth and development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Generic summarized core pathway gene class included because the review centers on TPS family functions.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; TPS-e/f subfamily diterpene synthase-related enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GGPP to (E,E)-geranyllinalool, the first dedicated precursor step toward TMTT formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in leaves locally at wound sites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Herbivore- and jasmonate-induced leaves; local wound sites
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: indirect defense via attraction of parasitoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes herbivore-induced TMTT biosynthesis in Arabidopsis leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional gene characterization; expression analysis; induced volatile profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS04 catalyzes formation of (E,E)-geranyllinalool. 2. This is the first dedicated step in TMTT biosynthesis. 3. AtTPS04 is induced by herbivory/jasmonate and contributes to indirect defense-related volatile emission.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering indirect insect defense through enhanced homoterpene emission
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: herbivory; elicitor treatment; jasmonic acid or jasmonate mimics; wounding
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray; transcript analysis; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible carbon cost and ecological tradeoffs from altered volatile blends not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as pathway gene because it catalyzes a dedicated metabolic step, though the review discusses it as an evolutionary exemplar.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in pollen and induced locally at wound sites in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: flowers and leaves; emitted as volatile
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: floral scent and herbivore-induced indirect defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces α-farnesene in floral and herbivore-induced volatile blends
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; expression analysis; volatile profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS03 encodes an α-farnesene synthase. 2. It contributes to floral volatile production and herbivore-induced leaf volatile emissions. 3. Together with AtTPS02, it exemplifies evolution of altered subcellular targeting and substrate use.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool to tailor volatile-mediated ecological interactions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Volatile blend changes may alter ecological interactions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review states AtTPS03 lacks a transit peptide and is cytosolic, unlike plastidial AtTPS02.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Responsible for predominant floral sesquiterpene emission in Arabidopsis flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; tissue-specific expression studies; volatile profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS21 is one of two major flower-specific sesquiterpene synthases in Arabidopsis. 2. It produces (E)-β-caryophyllene/α-humulene and contributes strongly to floral scent. 3. Its orthologue in Arabidopsis lyrata underlies lineage-specific volatile evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be exploited to modify floral scent or volatile-mediated defense
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core pathway exemplar in the review’s functional summary of Arabidopsis TPS genes.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts FPP into multiple sesquiterpene hydrocarbons in flowers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Flower specific; intrafloral nectaries and ovules
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: flowers, especially intrafloral nectaries and ovules
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: floral ecological interactions and possible defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates much of the complex floral sesquiterpene mixture in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; tissue-specific expression studies; volatile profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS11 is a major flower-specific multi-product sesquiterpene synthase. 2. It produces many floral sesquiterpenes in Arabidopsis. 3. Spatial expression in nectaries and ovules indicates tissue-specialized terpene metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to reprogram floral volatile bouquets
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered ecological interactions with pollinators or antagonists

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root vascular tissue of elongation zone and epidermal cells/root hairs of older root zones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces root 1,8-cineole in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS23 encodes a 1,8-cineole synthase expressed in specific root cell types. 2. It contributes to root monoterpene formation and likely rhizosphere interactions. 3. It is a recent duplicate of AtTPS27.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering rhizosphere-active volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtTPS23 and AtTPS27 are identical duplicated genes in a tandem cluster.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root vascular tissue of elongation zone and epidermal cells/root hairs of older root zones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces root 1,8-cineole in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS27 is identical to AtTPS23 and encodes a root-expressed 1,8-cineole synthase. 2. It exemplifies tandem duplication within the Arabidopsis TPS family. 3. Its localized expression supports cell type-specific root terpene metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering rhizosphere-active volatiles

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to root-specific sesquiterpene metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS12 encodes a root-expressed (Z)-γ-bisabolene synthase. 2. It is co-localized with related biosynthetic genes in putative root metabolic modules. 3. Gene duplication contributed to this pathway specialization.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for root defense engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Co-expressed with P450 genes CYP71A19 and CYP71A20 in putative cell type-specific modules.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: cortex and endodermis of mature roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to root-specific sesquiterpene metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS13 is a duplicated (Z)-γ-bisabolene synthase gene in Arabidopsis roots. 2. It exhibits complementary root cell type-specific expression relative to AtTPS12. 3. The duplicated pair likely forms part of specialized root sesquiterpene modules.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for root defense engineering

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: herbivore-induced leaves in ecotypes with active allele
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls ecotype-specific β-ocimene emission; inactive in Col due to mutation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative ecotype analysis; functional characterization; volatile profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS02 encodes a plastidial β-ocimene synthase. 2. A mutation in the Col ecotype inactivates AtTPS02 and explains ecotype-specific differences in volatile emission. 3. Together with AtTPS03 it illustrates neofunctionalization via subcellular targeting.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to restore or enhance indirect defense volatile blends
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as functionally important for natural variation.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces insect-induced germacrene D in poplar
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: full-length cDNA isolation; enzyme functional characterization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtdTPS1 encodes a sesquiterpene synthase for insect-induced (-)-germacrene D. 2. It contributes to herbivore-induced volatile emissions in poplar. 3. These emissions likely function in defense and signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering herbivore-responsive volatile defenses in trees

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts FPP to (-)-valencene and (-)-7-epi-α-selinene as main products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in anthers and developing pollen grains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: floral tissues with diurnal scent emission
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to grapevine floral scent and aroma-associated sesquiterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization of TPS cDNAs; expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A grapevine sesquiterpene synthase from Cabernet Sauvignon and Gewürztraminer produces valencene and 7-epi-α-selinene. 2. The gene is expressed in anthers and developing pollen. 3. It contributes to diurnal floral scent emission and grape aroma chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High value target for grape and wine aroma improvement
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review broadly summarizes 43 functionally characterized VvTPS FLcDNAs, but only this named example is explicit in the text.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; class II diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GGPP to ent-copalyl diphosphate for gibberellin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies precursor for gibberellin biosynthesis in rice
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCPS1 is one of three characterized rice CPS enzymes. 2. It functions in gibberellin biosynthesis. 3. It channels GGPP into ent-CPP for primary hormone metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation could alter plant growth through gibberellin levels

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; class II diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GGPP to ent-copalyl diphosphate for specialized non-gibberellin diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: phytocassanes / oryzalexins and related rice diterpenoid phytoalexins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of non-gibberellin defensive diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCPS2 supplies ent-CPP to rice specialized diterpenoid biosynthesis. 2. It is distinct from the gibberellin-related OsCPS1. 3. It underpins formation of defensive labdane-related diterpenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for enhancing rice disease resistance via phytoalexin production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible resource allocation tradeoffs not discussed

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; class II diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GGPP to syn-CPP for syn-labdane-related diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides syn-CPP precursor for syn-labdane diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCPSSyn produces syn-CPP in rice. 2. This enzyme feeds the biosynthesis of syn-labdane-related diterpenoids. 3. These downstream metabolites include defense compounds and allelochemicals.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for increasing momilactone/allelopathic diterpenoids in rice
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential impacts on neighboring plant interactions and resource use

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; class I diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-kaurene for gibberellin biosynthesis in rice
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKS1 is the bona fide rice kaurene synthase for gibberellin biosynthesis. 2. It converts ent-CPP to ent-kaurene. 3. It represents the primary metabolism KS branch within the TPS family.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be manipulated to alter GA-mediated growth, with likely developmental tradeoffs

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; class I diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Channels syn-CPP into syn-pimaradiene-derived diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKSL4/OsDTS2 converts syn-CPP to syn-pimaradiene. 2. It is one of several rice KSL enzymes diversifying labdane-related diterpenes. 3. These products feed defensive specialized metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter defensive diterpenoid spectrum

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; class I diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to rice defensive diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKS5 is one of several rice class I diterpene synthases acting on ent-CPP. 2. It produces ent-pimara-8(14),15-diene. 3. Its products belong to the defensive non-gibberellin diterpenoid network.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential disease-resistance engineering target

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; class I diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to specialized defensive diterpenoid biosynthesis in rice
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKS6 produces ent-isokaurene from ent-CPP. 2. It is part of the diversified rice diterpene synthase network. 3. This network generates phytoalexin-related defensive metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential disease-resistance engineering target

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; class I diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes phytocassane biosynthesis by forming ent-cassadiene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKSL7/OsDTC1 produces ent-cassadiene. 2. This compound is the hydrocarbon precursor of phytocassane phytoalexins. 3. The pathway is central to rice antimicrobial defense chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering disease-resistant rice through elevated phytocassane pathway flux

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; class I diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Channels syn-CPP into syn-stemarene-derived defensive diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKSL8/OsDTC2 produces syn-stemarene from syn-CPP. 2. This enzyme supports a specialized branch of rice defensive diterpenoid metabolism. 3. Its products contribute to the diversity of rice labdane-related defenses.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter specialized phytoalexin profile

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; class I diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes oryzalexin pathway flux via ent-sandaracopimaradiene formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKSL10 produces ent-sandaracopimaradiene. 2. This hydrocarbon feeds oryzalexin biosynthesis. 3. It is part of the expanded rice TPS-e/f subfamily linked to defensive chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for enhancing disease-defense diterpenoid pathways in rice

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase; class I diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to stemodene-related diterpenoid metabolism in rice
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKSL11 converts syn-CPP to stemod-13(17)-ene. 2. It diversifies the rice syn-labdane diterpenoid network. 3. This branch contributes to the chemically rich defensive diterpenoid repertoire of rice.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for specialized diterpenoid engineering
